summary: Dan Shapiro recounts a dinner at Palisade, a Seattle seafood restaurant he recommends for tourists. He describes the restaurant's panoramic waterfront views, live fish displays, and excellent seafood. The core of the post is his discovery that the restaurant's extensive woodwork is made entirely of Koa, an expensive Hawaiian hardwood he had coincidentally just read about. He jokes about wanting a fish-it-yourself restaurant and closes with affectionate asides about his mother-in-law and his own small stash of Koa wood.
key_points: Palisade is recommended as Seattle's premier tourist restaurant for its views, live marine displays, and fresh seafood
The restaurant is extensively decorated with Koa, a rare and expensive Hawaiian hardwood at $30/board foot
Shapiro's woodworking knowledge let him identify the wood, and a coincidental article he'd read confirmed his guess
The post is an early personal blog entry showing Shapiro's interests in craft, materials, and maker culture before his later tech career prominence
money_quotes: That's what I want, a fish-it-yourself restaurant.
According to our server, the Maitre d' stand alone is $10k worth of lumber.
Great seafood, but not quite perfect–a tiny bit dumbed-down for the masses. Perfect for tourists.
I've got a 2 bf bit of koa in my garage just waiting for a project…
My mother-in-law is a delightful person who I enjoy spending time with. No, I am not being sarcastic. No, she does not read this weblog. Yes, I am very, very lucky.
key_insight: An early personal blog post revealing Shapiro's eye for craftsmanship and materials — interests that would later manifest in his founding of Glowforge.
